evals.prompt: I want to run a parameter sweep on dt and kappa for my phase-field simulation. Try 5 values of dt between 1e-4 and 1e-2, and 4 values of kappa between 0.1 and 1.0. My base config is simulation.json. Use linspace sampling.
evals.expected_output: Agent generates a sweep with 5x4=20 configurations using linspace method via sweep_generator.py, creating configs in the output directory.
assertions: Invokes scripts/sweep_generator.py with --base-config simulation.json --params 'dt:1e-4:1e-2:5,kappa:0.1:1.0:4' --method linspace --json
Specifies an --output-dir for the generated configurations
Output includes configs, parameter_space, sweep_method, and total_runs fields
Reports total_runs as 20 (5 x 4 combinations)

script_checks.description: 5x4 linspace sweep of dt and kappa yields 20 runs with exact grid values

evals.prompt: I have 4 parameters to explore: dt (1e-4 to 1e-2), kappa (0.1 to 1.0), M (1e-6 to 1e-4), and W (0.5 to 2.0). I can only afford 25 runs total. Generate a space-filling design.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses LHS method with 25 samples for the 4-parameter space since full factorial would require too many runs, invoking sweep_generator.py.
assertions: Invokes scripts/sweep_generator.py with --method lhs --samples 25 and all 4 parameter ranges
Selects LHS over grid because 4 parameters with grid would be exponential
Output includes 25 configuration files
Explains why LHS is preferred for 4-dimensional space with limited budget

script_checks.description: LHS over 4 params with budget 25 yields 25 runs; seed-42 sample values are deterministic

evals.prompt: Initialize a campaign for my sweep directory at ./dt_kappa_sweep. The simulation command is 'python phase_field.py --config {config}'. Then show me the status.
evals.expected_output: Agent initializes the campaign with campaign_manager.py and then checks status, reporting job counts and progress.
assertions: Invokes scripts/campaign_manager.py with --action init --config-dir ./dt_kappa_sweep --command 'python phase_field.py --config {config}' --json
Invokes scripts/campaign_manager.py with --action status --config-dir ./dt_kappa_sweep --json
The status invocation output includes campaign_id, status, jobs, and progress fields (the init invocation instead emits campaign_id, total_jobs, config_dir, command_template)
Reports the number of pending, running, and completed jobs

script_checks.description: init builds one job per config in the manifest and echoes the command template
status summarizes a mixed-state campaign (2 completed, 1 running, 1 pending -> progress 0.5, in_progress)

evals.prompt: All my simulations finished. Aggregate the results from ./dt_kappa_sweep and find which run minimized the interface_width metric.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs result_aggregator.py on the campaign directory to collect results and identify the best run by the interface_width metric.
assertions: Invokes scripts/result_aggregator.py with --campaign-dir ./dt_kappa_sweep --metric interface_width --json
Output includes summary, statistics, best_run, and failed_runs fields
Identifies the run with the minimum interface_width value and its parameters
Reports summary statistics (mean, std, min, max) across all runs

script_checks.description: minimize interface_width: best run is job_0001 (0.15); stats and failed run pinned

evals.prompt: Generate a single-parameter sweep: just vary dt from 1e-5 to 1e-3 with 10 evenly spaced values. Base config is config.json. Output to ./dt_study.
evals.expected_output: Agent generates a simple 1D sweep with 10 linspace values for dt only, appropriate for a focused single-parameter study.
assertions: Invokes scripts/sweep_generator.py with --base-config config.json --params 'dt:1e-5:1e-3:10' --method linspace --output-dir ./dt_study --json
Uses linspace method for uniform 1D sampling
Output shows total_runs as 10
Creates 10 configuration files with evenly spaced dt values

script_checks.description: 1D linspace sweep of dt with 10 points yields 10 runs and exact evenly-spaced values
